TweakWhy_AccountProtectionWarn: Windows Security shows a warning if you are not signed in with a Microsoft account. Disabling hides this recurring notification.
TweakWhy_ActiveHours: Defines the time range when Windows will not automatically restart your PC to finish installing updates.
TweakWhy_ActivityHistory: Records your activity across devices when signed into a Microsoft account and can sync it to the cloud. Disabling keeps activity local.
TweakWhy_AdminApprovalMode: Controls how User Account Control behaves when an app requests elevated privileges. Default prompts for consent, Never disables UAC prompts. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_AdobeNetworkBlock: Adds firewall rules to block Adobe applications from accessing the internet. This affects license validation, cloud sync, and Creative Cloud features. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_AdvancedStartupShortcut: Creates a desktop shortcut that immediately reboots the PC into the Advanced Startup Options (Windows Recovery Environment) menu.
TweakWhy_AdvertisingID: Windows assigns a unique advertising ID to your account that apps can use to show targeted ads. Disabling stops this tracking.
TweakWhy_AeroShaking: Shaking a window title bar minimises all other open windows. Disable if you find this triggers by accident during normal use.
TweakWhy_AppColorMode: Sets the default colour mode that applications use when they respect the system setting: Dark or Light.
TweakWhy_AppsLanguageSwitch: Allows Windows to remember and switch to a different input language or keyboard layout per application window.
TweakWhy_AppsSilentInstalling: Windows can silently install suggested apps in the background without asking you. Disabling stops this behaviour. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_AppsSmartScreen: SmartScreen checks downloaded files and apps against a cloud reputation database and warns you before running unknown executables. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_Autoplay: Controls whether Windows automatically opens a prompt or launches an app when you insert a USB drive or other removable media.
TweakWhy_AutoRebootOnCrash: When Windows crashes (blue screen), it can automatically restart. Disabling keeps the error on screen so you can read the stop code.
TweakWhy_BackgroundApps: Controls whether apps are allowed to run and update in the background when they are not in focus. Disabling saves CPU and memory. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_BingSearch: The Start Menu search box can show Bing web results alongside local results. Disabling keeps Start Menu search local only.
TweakWhy_BootRecovery: Controls whether Windows automatically enters recovery mode after consecutive failed boot attempts. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_BraveDebloat: Disables Brave browser's optional extras: rewards, crypto wallet, VPN, and AI chat. Only affects these Brave-specific features, not your browsing. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_BSoDStopError: Shows the specific error code on the blue screen of death instead of just the generic sad face emoji.
TweakWhy_BuildNumberOnDesktop: Displays the Windows build number and edition as a watermark in the bottom right corner of your desktop.
TweakWhy_CABInstallContext: Adds an Install option to the right-click context menu for .cab archive files.
TweakWhy_Camera: Master toggle controlling whether apps can request access to your camera. Disabling prevents all apps from using the camera. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_CapsLock: Controls whether the Caps Lock key is enabled. Disabling remaps the Caps Lock key so pressing it does nothing.
TweakWhy_ChangingSoundScheme: Controls whether users can change the Windows sound scheme. Disabling this locks the current scheme and prevents changes.
TweakWhy_CheckBoxes: Shows small checkboxes next to files and folders in Explorer to make it easier to select multiple items without holding Ctrl.
TweakWhy_CheckWinGet: WinGet is Windows' built-in package manager used to install and update apps from the command line. This ensures it is installed and up to date.
TweakWhy_CIMemoryIntegrity: Hypervisor-protected code integrity runs Windows kernel code in a virtualized environment to prevent kernel-level malware and rootkits. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_ClearRecentFiles: Automatically clears the recent files list each time you log out. Improves privacy on shared or multi-user machines.
TweakWhy_ClipboardHistory: Stores a history of copied items so you can paste from previous clips using Win+V. Enable to turn this feature on.
TweakWhy_CompressedFolderNewContext: Shows or hides the Compressed (zipped) Folder option in the right-click New submenu in File Explorer.
TweakWhy_ConnectionSharing: Allows this PC to share its internet connection with other devices, acting as a software hotspot. Enabling shows this option in network settings. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_ControlPanelView: Sets the default display style for Control Panel to Large Icons, Small Icons, or the default Category grouping.
TweakWhy_Copilot: Removes Copilot, Recall, and all related Windows AI components. Reinstalling requires winget or Microsoft Store access.
TweakWhy_CortanaAutostart: Prevents Cortana from launching automatically at startup. Cortana can still be opened manually if needed.
TweakWhy_CreateRestorePoint: Creates a Windows System Restore point so you can roll back system changes if something goes wrong. Recommended before running any tweaks.
TweakWhy_CrossDeviceResume: Lets you resume tasks from other Windows devices on this PC via a notification pop-up. Disable if you do not use this cross-device feature.
TweakWhy_CurrentNetwork: Sets the network profile for your currently active connection. Private enables network discovery and sharing, Public restricts it. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DefaultTerminalApp: Sets which terminal application opens when you launch Command Prompt or PowerShell from within Windows.
TweakWhy_DefenderAppGuard: Opens Edge browser tabs in an isolated virtual machine to prevent malicious websites from accessing the host system or its files. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DefenderCloud: Enables Microsoft Defender's cloud-based protection for real-time threat lookups using Microsoft's global threat intelligence database. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DefenderSandbox: Runs the Microsoft Defender antivirus engine inside an isolated sandbox process for extra protection. Adds a small performance overhead. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DefenderTrayIcon: Shows or hides the Windows Security (Defender) icon in the system tray. Hiding it does not disable Defender protection.
TweakWhy_DeliveryOptimization: Windows can use your PC to share Windows Update download data with other PCs on your network or the internet. Disabling keeps your downloads private. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DEPOptOut: Sets Data Execution Prevention to OptOut mode, enabling DEP protection for all processes except those you explicitly exclude. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DiagnosticDataLevel: Controls how much diagnostic data Windows sends to Microsoft. Minimal sends only basic device info and reliability data.
TweakWhy_DiagTrackService: The DiagTrack service collects and uploads Windows usage and diagnostic data to Microsoft servers. Disabling it stops background telemetry uploads. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableAESCiphers: Disables older weak AES cipher entries in the Windows SCHANNEL security layer. Forces applications to negotiate stronger cipher configurations. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableAutoRun: Prevents Windows from automatically running programs on USB drives and other removable media. Stops a common malware spread vector.
TweakWhy_DisableHashAlgorithms: Configures SCHANNEL hash algorithm settings. Disables MD5 and enables SHA family algorithms for stronger integrity verification. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableIPv6: Disables the IPv6 protocol stack system-wide. Recommended if your network does not use IPv6 to reduce unnecessary attack surface. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableRC2RC4Ciphers: Disables RC2 and RC4 ciphers in SCHANNEL. These algorithms are cryptographically broken and should not be used in any TLS connection. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableRemoteCommands: DCOM is a remote activation protocol required by enterprise management tools (SCCM, WMI). Disabling it reduces attack surface but may break remote management workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableSMBv3Compression: Turns off SMB compression to protect against CVE-2020-0796. Only affects SMB traffic compression, not file access speed. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableTCPTimestamps: Removes TCP timestamp information from network packets. Prevents remote systems from fingerprinting your OS based on uptime. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DisableTripleDESCipher: Disables Triple DES (3DES) in SCHANNEL. 3DES is vulnerable to SWEET32 birthday attacks and is no longer considered secure. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DiskCleanup: Runs the built-in Windows Disk Cleanup tool to remove temporary files, system cache, and other unneeded data to free up disk space. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DismissMSAccount: Removes the recurring prompt in Windows Security asking you to sign in with a Microsoft account for additional protection.
TweakWhy_DismissSmartScreenFilter: Removes the recurring prompt in Windows Security asking you to enable the SmartScreen reputation filter.
TweakWhy_DNSoverHTTPS: Encrypts DNS lookups by sending them over HTTPS instead of plain UDP. Prevents your ISP or network from monitoring or tampering with DNS traffic. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_DownloadBlocking: Controls whether Windows Security marks downloaded files as potentially unsafe using the Zone.Identifier alternate data stream.
TweakWhy_EdgeDebloat: Applies Group Policy settings to disable telemetry, shopping assistant, collections, Copilot sidebar, and other optional Edge extras. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_EditWithClipchampContext: Shows or hides the Edit with Clipchamp video editor option in the right-click context menu for video files.
TweakWhy_EditWithPaintContext: Shows or hides the Edit with Paint option in the right-click context menu for image files.
TweakWhy_EditWithPhotosContext: Shows or hides the Edit with Photos option in the right-click context menu for image files.
TweakWhy_EnableBiometricsAntiSpoofing: Enables enhanced anti-spoofing for Windows Hello facial recognition. Only applies if you have compatible biometric hardware.
TweakWhy_EnableSMBGuestCompatibility: Allows SMB connections to network shares without requiring authentication. Needed for accessing older NAS devices, printers, or routers. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_EncCompFilesColor: Shows encrypted files in green and compressed files in blue in File Explorer. Makes these file types immediately identifiable.
TweakWhy_EnhPointerPrecision: Mouse acceleration that adjusts cursor speed based on how fast you physically move the mouse. Disable for consistent raw mouse input, recommended for gaming.
TweakWhy_ErrorReporting: When an application crashes, Windows Error Reporting can send a report to Microsoft. Disabling stops this data from leaving your machine.
TweakWhy_EventViewerCustomView: Creates a custom view in Event Viewer filtered to show process creation events, making it easier to audit what programs have been launched.
TweakWhy_ExplorerAutoDiscovery: File Explorer detects the content type of each folder and applies a matching view template. Disabling locks all folders to a consistent generic view.
TweakWhy_ExplorerTitleFullPath: Shows the complete folder path in the File Explorer title bar instead of just the folder name. Useful when working with many folders of the same name.
TweakWhy_F1HelpPage: Pressing F1 in File Explorer or on the desktop opens the Windows help website in your browser. Disabling prevents this browser launch.
TweakWhy_F8BootMenu: Enables the legacy F8 key boot menu that lets you select Safe Mode and other startup options during the Windows boot process.
TweakWhy_FastStartup: A hybrid shutdown mode that saves the kernel state to disk so Windows boots faster. Can cause issues with dual-boot setups or full shutdowns.
TweakWhy_FeedbackFrequency: Controls how often Windows asks you to rate your experience or submit feedback. Setting to Never stops all feedback prompts.
TweakWhy_FileDeleteConfirm: Shows a confirmation dialog before permanently deleting a file. Enable to get a prompt before files are sent to the Recycle Bin.
TweakWhy_FileExplorerCompactMode: Reduces the spacing between items in File Explorer for a denser, more information-rich list view.
TweakWhy_FileExtensions: Shows the file extension (e.g. .txt, .exe) after every filename in Explorer. Helps identify what type a file is at a glance.
TweakWhy_FileOperationsDetails: Shows the detailed progress dialog for file operations (copy, move, delete) by default instead of the minimal progress bar.
TweakWhy_FileTransferDialog: Controls whether the file copy and move dialog shows full details like speed and time remaining by default.
TweakWhy_Firewall: Enables or disables Windows Defender Firewall across all network profiles. Disabling leaves all inbound ports completely unfiltered. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_FirstLogonAnimation: Shows a brief welcome animation and the Hi screen the first time you sign in after a major Windows update.
TweakWhy_FldrSeparateProcess: Opens each Explorer folder window in its own process. If one crashes, the others stay open. Uses slightly more memory.
TweakWhy_FolderGroupBy: Controls whether files in the Downloads folder are grouped by date modified. Setting to None shows all files in a flat list.
TweakWhy_FullscreenOptimizations: A Windows feature that optimises games and apps running in fullscreen mode for better performance. Disabling can fix compatibility issues in some older games.
TweakWhy_GameDVR: Background recording continuously captures gameplay so you can save the last few minutes after something happens. Disabling frees GPU and disk resources for better FPS consistency.
TweakWhy_GPUScheduling: Hardware-accelerated GPU scheduling lets the GPU manage its own video memory directly, potentially reducing input latency in games.
TweakWhy_Hibernation: Saves the entire contents of RAM to disk and powers the PC off completely. The hibernation file can use several gigabytes of disk space.
TweakWhy_HiddenItems: Shows files and folders that are marked as hidden in File Explorer. Hidden items appear slightly transparent when visible.
TweakWhy_HomeGroups: Home Groups was a Windows feature for sharing files between home PCs. It was removed in Windows 10 1803 but residual services may still be present.
TweakWhy_ImportExploitProtectionPolicy: Downloads and imports a pre-built exploit mitigation policy file into Windows Defender Exploit Guard, applying custom process mitigations. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_InputMethod: Sets the default keyboard input method Windows uses. English forces the English layout regardless of other installed languages.
TweakWhy_JPEGWallpapersQuality: Windows compresses JPEG wallpapers when setting them as the desktop background. Setting to Max preserves full image quality.
TweakWhy_LanguageListAccess: Allows websites to read your Windows language preference list to tailor content. Disable to prevent websites from detecting your language settings.
TweakWhy_LanmanWorkstationGuestAuthPolic: Guest authentication for SMB is a known lateral-movement vector disabled by default since Windows 10 1709. Enabling it exposes the system to unauthenticated SMB access. Only enable for legacy device compatibility.
TweakWhy_LatestInstalled.NET: Forces 32-bit and 64-bit applications to use the newest installed .NET runtime version instead of the version they were originally built against.
TweakWhy_LLMNR: Link-Local Multicast Name Resolution resolves hostnames on local networks. It can be exploited in credential capture attacks and is best disabled. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_LocalSecurityAuthority: Runs the Windows LSA process in protected mode to prevent credential theft by tools like Mimikatz. Recommended for all systems. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_LocationService: Controls whether apps can access your device's geographic location. Disabling prevents location tracking by all apps. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_LockScreen: Controls whether the Windows lock screen is displayed before the sign-in screen on Windows 11.
TweakWhy_LockScreenBlur: Applies an acrylic blur effect to the sign-in screen background. Disabling shows the background without blurring.
TweakWhy_LockScreenRS1: Controls whether the Windows lock screen is displayed before the sign-in screen on Windows 10 (RS1 era policy path).
TweakWhy_LockWidgets: The Windows Web Experience Pack adds widgets and dynamic content to the lock screen and taskbar. Disabling removes these.
TweakWhy_MaintenanceWakeUp: Windows can wake your PC from sleep overnight to run automatic maintenance tasks. Disabling prevents unwanted overnight wake-ups.
TweakWhy_MapUpdates: Windows Maps can automatically download updated offline map data in the background. Disabling saves bandwidth if you do not use the Maps app.
TweakWhy_MeetNow: Meet Now icon in the notification area
TweakWhy_MergeConflicts: Shows a warning when merging folders that contain files with the same name. Disable to silently overwrite files without a prompt.
TweakWhy_Microphone: Master toggle controlling whether apps can request access to your microphone. Disabling prevents all apps from using the mic. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_MostUsedApps: Shows your most frequently launched apps at the top of the Start Menu app list for quick access.
TweakWhy_MouseAcceleration: Enhance Pointer Precision scales mouse movement based on speed. Disabling gives a 1:1 raw input ratio preferred by competitive FPS players for consistent aim.
TweakWhy_MSIExtractContext: Adds an Extract option to the right-click context menu for .msi installer files, letting you browse their contents without running the installer.
TweakWhy_MSNetClient: The Microsoft Networks client that allows this PC to access files and printers shared by other Windows computers on the network. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_MultiplaneOverlay: A GPU compositing feature that can improve performance. Disabling can fix visual glitches or flickering in some display configurations.
TweakWhy_MultipleInvokeContext: When selecting 15 or more files and right-clicking, Windows normally shows a warning before displaying the context menu. This raises or removes that limit.
TweakWhy_NaglesAlgorithm: Reduces network buffering so packets are sent immediately. Can lower latency in competitive multiplayer, but may affect connection stability on some networks. Disable if you notice connection drops.
TweakWhy_NavigationPaneExpand: The Explorer navigation pane automatically expands and scrolls to highlight the folder you are currently viewing.
TweakWhy_NavPaneAllFolders: Shows all local folders including hidden system folders in the Explorer left navigation pane.
TweakWhy_NavPaneLibraries: Shows the Libraries section (Documents, Music, Pictures, Videos) in the Explorer left navigation pane.
TweakWhy_NCSIProbe: Network Connectivity Status Indicator checks connectivity by pinging Microsoft servers. Disabling may show incorrect network status icons. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_NetBIOS: NetBIOS is an older name resolution protocol used for Windows file sharing. Disabling on modern networks reduces unnecessary broadcast traffic. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_NetDevicesAutoInst: Controls whether Windows automatically downloads and installs drivers for newly connected network devices.
TweakWhy_NetworkAdaptersSavePower: Allows Windows to put network adapters into a low-power state when idle. Disabling prevents brief reconnection delays after the adapter wakes up.
TweakWhy_NetworkDiscovery: Enables this PC to find other devices on the local network and be visible to them. Required for browsing network shares in File Explorer. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_NetworkFromLockScreen: Controls whether the network selection button is visible on the lock screen before a user has signed in.
TweakWhy_NetworkProtection: Microsoft Defender's network protection blocks connections to known malicious domains and IP addresses at the network level. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_NewAppPrompt: When you try to open an unassociated file type, Windows shows a dialog asking how to open it. Disabling suppresses this prompt.
TweakWhy_NewOutlook: Controls whether the new Outlook app is available. Disable to keep using the classic Mail and Calendar apps instead.
TweakWhy_NewsInterests: News and weather content on the taskbar
TweakWhy_Notifications: Master switch for the entire Windows notification system. Disabling stops all app notifications and hides the notification centre. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_NTFSLastAccess: Updates a timestamp on every file each time it is read. Disabling reduces disk write activity on systems with many small file reads.
TweakWhy_NTFSLongPaths: Removes the 260-character limit on file paths. Required for some development tools and deeply nested folder structures.
TweakWhy_NumLock: Controls whether the Num Lock key is automatically turned on when Windows starts. Enable to always start with the number pad active.
TweakWhy_OneDrive: Installs the OneDrive desktop sync client, or removes the app and its Explorer integration. Uninstall is a package removal workflow, not a simple toggle, and bringing it back may require rerunning setup, signing in again, and waiting for sync to recover.
TweakWhy_OneDriveFileExplorerAd: OneDrive shows a promotional notification inside File Explorer encouraging you to sync files. Hiding removes this banner.
TweakWhy_OpenFileExplorerTo: Sets the default location File Explorer opens to when launched from the taskbar or Start Menu.
TweakWhy_OpenWindowsTerminalAdminContext: Open Windows Terminal as admin by default
TweakWhy_PerformanceTuning: Runs a set of legacy system optimisations and boot configuration adjustments to improve general system responsiveness. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_PowerPlan: Sets the Windows power plan. Balanced scales performance based on load. High Performance keeps the CPU at full speed. Ultimate Performance removes all power-saving throttling for maximum throughput. Ultimate Performance may not be available on all systems.
TweakWhy_Powershell7Telemetry: PowerShell 7 sends anonymous usage data to Microsoft by default. Disabling opts out of this telemetry. Linked to the Install/Update PowerShell 7 option.
TweakWhy_PowerShellModulesLogging: Logs every command run by PowerShell modules to the Windows event log. Useful for security auditing and investigating incidents.
TweakWhy_PowerShellScriptsLogging: Logs the full content of every PowerShell script block that executes to the Windows event log. Helps with post-incident investigations.
TweakWhy_PreventEdgeShortcutCreation: Stops Microsoft Edge from recreating its desktop shortcut after updates. Applies to all Edge release channels.
TweakWhy_PrintCMDContext: Shows or hides the Print option in the right-click context menu for .bat and .cmd script files.
TweakWhy_ProtectClickOnce: Disables ClickOnce trust prompts for all security zones. Prevents untrusted ClickOnce applications from prompting for installation. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ProtectFileSystemPerformance: Disables 8.3 short filename generation on NTFS. Improves filesystem performance and removes a legacy compatibility feature rarely needed today.
TweakWhy_ProtectMSOffice: Restricts macros, external content execution, and Outlook trust settings across Office versions. Reduces document-based attack vectors. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ProtectMSOfficeLinks: Prevents Word from automatically refreshing external links in documents and email. Reduces the risk of data exfiltration via embedded document links. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ProtectOS: Applies a broad set of registry hardening values covering credential protection, UAC, NTLM, TCP/IP, and smart card removal behaviour. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ProtectRPCSurface: Disables RPC-over-TCP for Task Scheduler and the remote Service Control Manager. Reduces exposure for remote management attack paths. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ProtectWinRM: Disables unencrypted WinRM traffic and digest authentication, then restarts the WinRM service. Ensures remote management connections are always encrypted. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_PrtScnSnippingTool: Makes the Print Screen key open the Snipping Tool for interactive screen capture instead of silently copying to the clipboard.
TweakWhy_PUAppsDetection: Enables Defender to detect and quarantine potentially unwanted applications such as adware, bundleware, and browser hijackers. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_QoS: The Quality of Service packet scheduler can prioritise certain types of network traffic. Required for some network QoS features to work correctly.
TweakWhy_QuickAccessFrequentFolders: The Quick Access section in Explorer can show frequently visited folders. Hide to keep Quick Access showing only your pinned folders.
TweakWhy_QuickAccessRecentFiles: The Quick Access section in Explorer can show recently opened files. Hide to keep Quick Access showing only your pinned folders.
TweakWhy_RazerBlock: Blocks Razer Synapse installer scripts from running automatically. Prevents unwanted Razer software from being installed in the background. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_RecentFiles: Controls whether Windows tracks recently accessed files for display in Quick Access and Open dialogs. Disable to stop this tracking.
TweakWhy_RecentlyAddedApps: Shows a list of recently installed apps in the Start Menu. Disable to keep the Start Menu app list cleaner.
TweakWhy_RecentShortcuts: Shows recently and frequently accessed files and folders as shortcuts in the Explorer Quick Access section.
TweakWhy_RecommendedTroubleshooting: Controls whether Windows automatically runs troubleshooters when problems are detected. Default still asks for your permission before running.
TweakWhy_RecycleBinDeleteConfirmation: Shows a confirmation prompt before moving files to the Recycle Bin. Enable to prevent accidental deletion.
TweakWhy_RegistryBackup: Enables automatic periodic backup of the Windows registry hive files to the RegBack folder inside the Windows directory.
TweakWhy_RepairSharedPrinterConnectionEr: Fixes common host and client-side printer connection failures by aligning RPC, SMB, Point and Print, spooler, TCP, and network discovery settings. This change carries moderate risk and requires a restart.
TweakWhy_RepairWindows11SMBUpdateIssue: Fixes a known issue in some Windows 11 builds where SMB share access fails after an update due to a missing registry value.
TweakWhy_ReservedStorage: Windows reserves several gigabytes of disk space for updates, temporary files, and caches. Disabling reclaims this reserved space after the next update. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_RestartDeviceAfterUpdate: Allows Windows to schedule a restart as soon as possible after completing an update installation.
TweakWhy_RestartNotification: Shows a notification in the taskbar when a Windows Update has been installed and requires a restart to complete.
TweakWhy_RestoreFldrWindows: Reopens any Explorer folder windows that were open when you last logged off. Restores your working context on each login.
TweakWhy_RevertStartMenu: Reverts the Start Menu to the pre-24H2 layout on Windows 11 24H2 builds. May affect compatibility with future Windows updates. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_S3Sleep: S3 is traditional deep sleep where the PC fully powers down components. Some systems default to Modern Standby which can drain power while idle.
TweakWhy_SaveRestartableApps: Windows can save the state of open apps when you sign out and reopen them automatically after your next sign in.
TweakWhy_SaveZoneInformation: When you download a file from the internet, Windows tags it with a zone marker. Keeping this enabled allows security warnings when running downloaded files.
TweakWhy_ScheduledTasks: Several scheduled tasks run in the background to collect and upload diagnostic data. Disabling stops these tasks from running automatically. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SearchAppInStore: When you open a file with an unknown extension, Windows can suggest looking in the Microsoft Store. Disabling skips this suggestion.
TweakWhy_SelectCheckboxes: Adds checkboxes next to files and folders in Explorer for easier multi-selection without needing to hold Ctrl.
TweakWhy_Sensors: Controls access to hardware sensors like accelerometer, gyroscope, and ambient light sensor. Mainly relevant for tablets and convertible devices.
TweakWhy_ServicesManual: Sets a selection of non-essential Windows services from Automatic to Manual startup. Services will still start on demand when needed. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SetDefenderExploitGuardPolicy: Applies additional Exploit Guard attack surface reduction rules via Windows Defender to block common malware techniques. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_SetDLLHijackingPrevention: Configures Session Manager DLL search order to prevent applications from loading DLLs from untrusted locations. Closes a common malware delivery path. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SetLOLBinFirewallRules: Adds outbound firewall block rules for built-in Windows binaries commonly abused by malware (LOLBins) to phone home or download payloads. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_SetNtpServerOverride: Overrides Windows Time to use pool.ntp.org instead of the default Windows time server.
TweakWhy_SetSMBSharingCompatibility: Preserves your existing SMB share configuration and saved network credentials when other network tweaks are applied. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SettingsSuggestedContent: The Settings app can show sponsored suggestions and tips alongside your settings. Hiding removes this promotional content.
TweakWhy_SetWindowsFirewallLogging: Enables logging of dropped and allowed connections to the Windows Firewall log file so you can audit network activity.
TweakWhy_SharedExperiences: Allows Windows to share activity between your devices via your Microsoft account. Disabling keeps activity local to this machine only.
TweakWhy_ShareMenu: Adds a Share option to the right-click context menu for files, letting you share them via apps or Windows nearby sharing.
TweakWhy_SharingMappedDrives: Allows mapped network drives to be visible to elevated (administrator) processes as well as the current logged-in user. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SharingWizard: Enables the simplified file sharing wizard when you right-click a file or folder and choose to share it.
TweakWhy_ShortcutArrow: Shows or hides the small arrow overlay icon on shortcut files to indicate they are shortcuts and not the original files.
TweakWhy_ShortcutsSuffix: Controls whether Windows appends - Shortcut to the name of new shortcuts created on the desktop.
TweakWhy_ShutdownFromLockScreen: Controls whether the shutdown and restart buttons are visible on the Windows lock screen.
TweakWhy_SigninInfo: Uses your sign-in credentials to automatically finish setting up your profile after a Windows update. Disable for more privacy.
TweakWhy_SleepButton: Controls whether the Sleep option appears in the Start Menu and whether the sleep keyboard button is active.
TweakWhy_SleepTimeout: Controls when the display turns off and when the PC enters sleep mode after inactivity. Disable to prevent automatic sleep.
TweakWhy_SMB1: SMBv1 is an outdated and vulnerable file sharing protocol known for EternalBlue exploits. Disable unless required for very old network devices. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SMBServer: Enables the SMB file server component that lets other computers on the network access shared folders and printers on this PC. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SnapAssist: When you snap a window to one side, Windows suggests other open windows to fill the remaining space. Disable to snap without suggestions.
TweakWhy_StandbyFix: Modern Standby (S0) can cause high CPU and power usage while the PC appears to be sleeping. This fix switches to S3-style deep sleep behaviour.
TweakWhy_StartAccountNotifications: Microsoft can show account-related notifications and prompts in the Start Menu. Hiding removes these.
TweakWhy_StartLayout: Controls the layout ratio of the Windows 11 Start Menu between pinned apps and recommended content.
TweakWhy_StartMenuAllSectionCategories: All section with categories in Start (24H2+)
TweakWhy_StartRecommendationsTips: The Start Menu can show tips about Windows features and recommendations for new apps. Hiding removes these suggestions.
TweakWhy_StartRecommendedSection: Shows or hides the Recommended section at the bottom of the Start Menu. On Enterprise and Education editions this can be hidden via policy.
TweakWhy_StartupSound: Plays the Windows startup chime when the desktop loads after you log in.
TweakWhy_StickyShift: Pressing Shift five times quickly triggers the Sticky Keys accessibility dialog. Disable to prevent this from interrupting your work.
TweakWhy_StorageSense: Automatically frees up disk space on a schedule by deleting temporary files, old downloads, and Recycle Bin contents.
TweakWhy_Superfetch: SysMain preloads frequently used apps into RAM to speed up launch times. On SSDs it provides little benefit and can usually be safely disabled. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SuperHiddenFiles: Shows protected Windows system files in File Explorer. These are hidden by default to prevent accidental modification or deletion.
TweakWhy_SuspendAirstrikeAttack: Hides the network selection UI from the Windows lock screen. Prevents changing network settings before signing in. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_SyncNotifications: OneDrive and other sync providers can show promotional notifications inside Explorer. Disable to hide these banners.
TweakWhy_TailoredExperiences: Uses your diagnostic data to show personalized tips, ads, and recommendations from Microsoft. Disabling opts you out.
TweakWhy_TaskbarEndTask: Add End Task to the taskbar context menu
TweakWhy_TaskManagerDetails: Opens Task Manager directly to the Details tab showing all processes instead of the simplified summary view.
TweakWhy_TaskViewButton: Task View button on the taskbar
TweakWhy_Teredo: Teredo is a tunneling protocol that provides IPv6 connectivity over IPv4 networks. Required for Xbox Live connectivity and some peer-to-peer applications. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_ThisPC: Shows or hides the This PC icon on your desktop for quick access to drives and devices.
TweakWhy_ThumbnailCache: Windows saves thumbnail previews in a hidden database file to speed up folder browsing. Disabling prevents this cache from being stored on disk.
TweakWhy_Thumbnails: Shows image and video previews as thumbnails in Explorer instead of generic file type icons. Disabling shows icons only.
TweakWhy_ThumbsDBOnNetwork: Windows creates hidden Thumbs.db files on network shares to cache thumbnails. Disabling prevents these files from being created on network locations.
TweakWhy_TitleBarColor: Colors window title bars to match your Windows accent colour instead of showing them in plain white or grey.
TweakWhy_TrayIcons: Shows all notification area icons in the system tray instead of hiding overflow icons behind the expand arrow.
TweakWhy_UnknownNetworks: Sets the default network profile applied to new or unrecognized network connections when they are first detected. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UpdateAdobereaderDCSTIG: Applies a set of security policy settings for Adobe Reader DC including protected mode, protected view, and disabling cloud and web features. This is a high-risk change that may be difficult or impossible to reverse.
TweakWhy_UpdateAutoDownload: Controls whether Windows Update automatically downloads updates in the background. Disabling lets you choose when to download updates. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UpdateCipherSuites: Sets the preferred SCHANNEL cipher suites to strong AES-256 and AES-128 variants. Ensures strong encryption for all TLS connections. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UpdateDesktopRegistry: Removes the small info icon that appears on the desktop when Windows Spotlight wallpapers are active.
TweakWhy_UpdateDotNetStrongAuth: Forces .NET Framework 4.x and 2.x to use system-default TLS and strong cryptography. Prevents older .NET apps from falling back to weak ciphers. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UpdateDriver: Controls whether Windows Update automatically downloads and installs hardware drivers. Disabling lets you manage drivers manually. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UpdateEventLogSize: Increases the Windows Security event log maximum size to 1 MB. Helps retain more audit events before older entries are overwritten.
TweakWhy_UpdateKeyExchanges: Enables Diffie-Hellman, ECDH, and PKCS key exchange algorithms in SCHANNEL. Ensures forward-secure key exchange is available for TLS connections. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UpdateMicrosoftProducts: When enabled, Windows Update also checks for and installs updates for other installed Microsoft software alongside Windows updates.
TweakWhy_UpdateMSProducts: When enabled, Windows Update also checks for updates to other Microsoft products like Office. Disabling limits updates to Windows only.
TweakWhy_UpdateMSRT: Controls whether Windows Update offers the Malicious Software Removal Tool scan. Disabling prevents it from being pushed via Windows Update. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UpdatePowershell: PowerShell 7 is a modern cross-platform shell with improved performance and features over the built-in Windows PowerShell 5.1. Linked to the PS7 Telemetry toggle.
TweakWhy_UpdateProtocols: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated with known vulnerabilities. Disabling them hardens the system against BEAST, POODLE, and related attacks. Re-enabling should only be done for legacy compatibility.
TweakWhy_UpdateRegistryPaths: Creates any missing registry paths required by other hardening tweaks before they run. This is a prerequisite step with no visible user-facing effect.
TweakWhy_UpdateRestart: Prevents Windows from automatically restarting after installing updates without your input. Gives you control over when reboots happen.
TweakWhy_UseStoreOpenWith: Shows or hides the Look for an app in the Microsoft Store option that appears in the Open With dialog for unassociated file types.
TweakWhy_UWPAccountInfo: Controls whether UWP apps can access your account name, picture, and other account information.
TweakWhy_UWPApps: Opens a selection dialog to remove or restore selected Windows Store apps. Some removals are one-way, and even supported restore paths can still require Microsoft Store, winget, or manual reinstall follow-up. This is a high-risk package operation rather than a simple toggle.
TweakWhy_UWPCalendar: Controls whether UWP apps can read and write to your calendar data.
TweakWhy_UWPCallHistory: Controls whether UWP apps can read your call history.
TweakWhy_UWPContacts: Controls whether UWP apps can access your contacts list.
TweakWhy_UWPDiagInfo: Controls whether UWP apps can collect diagnostic information about your device and its state.
TweakWhy_UWPEmail: Controls whether UWP apps can read and send email on your behalf.
TweakWhy_UWPFileSystem: Controls whether UWP apps can access files anywhere on your file system beyond their own app folders. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UWPMessaging: Controls whether UWP apps can read and send text messages.
TweakWhy_UWPNotifications: Controls whether UWP apps can send notifications to the Windows notification centre.
TweakWhy_UWPOtherDevices: Controls whether UWP apps can access other paired devices not covered by more specific categories.
TweakWhy_UWPPhoneCalls: Controls whether UWP apps can make and manage phone calls.
TweakWhy_UWPRadios: Controls whether UWP apps can control Bluetooth and other wireless radio devices.
TweakWhy_UWPSwapFile: The UWP swap file (swapfile.sys) is used by Windows Store apps for virtual memory paging. Disabling frees disk space if you do not use Store apps. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_UWPTasks: Controls whether UWP apps can access your task and to-do lists.
TweakWhy_UWPVoiceActivation: Controls whether UWP apps can be activated using voice commands in the background.
TweakWhy_VerboseStatus: Shows detailed status messages during Windows startup and shutdown instead of just the generic loading spinner.
TweakWhy_VisualFX: Controls the overall visual effects setting. Performance disables animations for a snappier feel. Appearance enables all effects.
TweakWhy_WAPPush: The WAP Push messaging service receives over-the-air messages from mobile carriers. Rarely needed on a PC not connected to a mobile network.
TweakWhy_WebLangList: Syncs your preferred language list to a Microsoft server so web content can match your preferences. Disable for more privacy.
TweakWhy_WebSearch: Integrates Bing web results into the Windows search bar. Disabling makes system search show only local files and settings.
TweakWhy_WhatsNewInWindows: Shows banners and prompts highlighting new Windows features after updates. Disable to suppress these notifications.
TweakWhy_WiFiSense: WiFi Sense can automatically share your WiFi password with your contacts. Disabling prevents this sharing behaviour.
TweakWhy_Win32LongPathLimit: Removes the 260-character maximum file path limit for Win32 applications. Required for some development tools and complex folder structures.
TweakWhy_WindowsCapabilities: Opens the optional features dialog to add or remove Windows capabilities such as language packs or RSAT tools. Recovery may require Windows Update access, source files, or restart follow-up, so this should be treated as package management rather than a simple toggle.
TweakWhy_WindowsColorMode: Sets whether the Windows shell (taskbar, Start Menu, action centre) uses Dark or Light mode.
TweakWhy_WindowsFeatures: Opens the Turn Windows features on or off dialog to enable or disable optional Windows components like Hyper-V or Telnet. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_WindowsGameMode: This controls the built-in Windows Game Mode setting, not Baseline's Gaming profile workflow. Windows Game Mode prioritises game processes and suppresses background activity. Some users disable it for streaming or recording stability where the priority changes can cause encoder drops.
TweakWhy_WindowsLatestUpdate: Enrolls this PC to receive Windows updates as soon as they are released rather than waiting for a gradual rollout. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_WindowsManageDefaultPrinter: Lets Windows automatically set your most recently used printer as the default. Disabling keeps your manually chosen default printer fixed.
TweakWhy_WindowsSandbox: A lightweight isolated virtual machine for safely running untrusted applications without risking your main system. Requires Windows Pro or higher.
TweakWhy_WindowsScriptHost: Controls whether Windows can run .vbs and .js script files. Disabling prevents these script types from executing when double-clicked. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_WindowsTips: Windows shows pop-up tips and suggestions while you work. Disabling stops these interruptions.
TweakWhy_WindowsWelcomeExperience: Shows a full-screen welcome page after major Windows updates highlighting new features. Disable to skip this screen.
TweakWhy_WPBT: WPBT is a firmware table that lets manufacturers run software during Windows startup. Disabling prevents manufacturer-injected executables from running automatically. This change carries moderate risk and may affect some workflows.
TweakWhy_XboxGameBar: The Xbox Game Bar is an in-game overlay (Win+G) for screenshots, video recording, and system performance monitoring.
TweakWhy_XboxGameTips: Shows onboarding tips and hints inside the Xbox Game Bar overlay. Disable if you are already familiar with Game Bar.

TweakWinDefault_DismissMSAccount: Defender MS account sign-in offer shown

TweakWinDefault_EdgeDebloat: Edge debloat policies are not applied
TweakWinDefault_EditWithClipchampContext: Edit with Clipchamp shown in context menu
TweakWinDefault_EditWithPaintContext: Edit with Paint shown in context menu
TweakWinDefault_EditWithPhotosContext: Edit with Photos shown in context menu
TweakWinDefault_EventViewerCustomView: Process Creation custom view not present in Event Viewer
TweakWinDefault_ExplorerAutoDiscovery: Explorer automatic folder type discovery is disabled
TweakWinDefault_ExplorerTitleFullPath: Full path not shown in Explorer title bar
TweakWinDefault_F8BootMenu: Legacy F8 boot menu is disabled
TweakWinDefault_FileDeleteConfirm: No confirmation shown when deleting files
TweakWinDefault_FileExplorerCompactMode: File Explorer compact mode is disabled
TweakWinDefault_FileOperationsDetails: File operations shown in compact view
TweakWinDefault_FileTransferDialog: File transfer dialog shown in compact mode
TweakWinDefault_FirstLogonAnimation: First sign-in animation after upgrade is enabled
TweakWinDefault_FldrSeparateProcess: Folder windows share the same process
TweakWinDefault_FolderGroupBy: Downloads folder grouped by date modified
